stories.title: Series Preview — Cubs Host Rockies (Game 1 of 3, Wrigley, 7:05 PM CT)
stories.angle: Mandatory series-start preview. Cubs (37-35) open 3-game home stand vs Colorado (27-45). Imanaga vs Lorenzen (2-8, 7.54 ERA). Wild card implications for both teams (Cubs yes, Rockies no).

x_posts.label: Post 1A
x_posts.text:
Cubs open a 3-game home stand vs Colorado (27-45) at Wrigley — 7:05 PM CT.

Imanaga vs. Lorenzen (2-8, 7.54 ERA). The Cubs are the heavy favorite for a reason.

37-35 and hunting a wild card. Good teams beat bad teams.

#Cubs #GoCubs #FlyTheW

stories.title: Giants Series Finale Recap — Cubs Fall 5-1, Still Win Road Trip 2-1
stories.angle: Logan Webb pitched 8 shutout innings, 7 K in the Giants' 5-1 win June 14. Colin Rea allowed 4 runs in 4 2/3 IP. Silver lining: Cubs took 2 of 3 in SF and return home 37-35. Road trip was net positive.

x_posts.label: Post 2A
x_posts.text:
Cubs dropped the SF finale — Giants 5-1, Logan Webb dominant for 8 innings.

Rea gave up 4 runs in 4 2/3 IP. Webb made everyone look helpless.

Still: 2-of-3 won on the road. Cubs come home 37-35 and immediately face the 27-45 Rockies. Chapter closed.

#Cubs #CubsBaseball #MLB

research.facts:
Giants Series Finale Recap
- **[HIGH]** Final score: Giants 5, Cubs 1 (June 14, 2026) — MLB.com Gameday, ESPN recap, Bleacher Nation enhanced box score
- **[HIGH]** Logan Webb pitched 8 innings, allowed 7 hits, 0 walks, 7 strikeouts — ESPN recap
- **[HIGH]** Colin Rea allowed 4 runs in 4 2/3 innings — ESPN recap (Rea used after opener Ryan Rolison threw 11 pitches)
- **[HIGH]** Giants' key hits: Lee scored via sac bunt sequence, Drew Gilbert RBI double, Matt Chapman 7th HR, Bryce Eldridge RBI single — ESPN/CBS recap
- **[HIGH]** Cubs won games 1 and 2 of SF series (5-1 June 12, 6-1 June 13) — ESPN game IDs confirmed
- **[HIGH]** Cubs went 2-1 in the series, come home 37-35 — derived from confirmed games

stories.title: Cubs Rotation Crisis & Trade Deadline Alert
stories.angle: Cubs starters 2-12, 7.11 ERA in last 20 games — last in MLB. 49 days to the Aug 3 deadline. Jed Hoyer confirmed shopping. Casey Mize (Tigers) and Reid Detmers (Angels) among emerging targets.

x_posts.label: Post 3A
x_posts.text:
Cubs' starters: 2-12, 7.11 ERA in their last 20 games.

DEAD LAST in baseball.

49 days to the deadline. Hoyer is shopping. Casey Mize and Reid Detmers are among the names. The rotation needs reinforcements — and it needs them fast.

#Cubs #MLB #CubsBaseball

research.facts:
Cubs Rotation Crisis & Trade Deadline Alert
- **[MEDIUM]** Cubs starters 2-12, 7.11 ERA in last 20 games, ranks last in MLB — ChiCitySports/CubbiesCrib trade deadline articles (blog-level reporting, not primary box score data). Cross-reference needed.
- **[HIGH]** Trade deadline: August 3, 2026 (49 days from June 15) — calculated. June 14 story-history confirmed "50-day countdown to August 3 deadline" from June 14 pipeline.
- **[MEDIUM]** Jed Hoyer confirmed Cubs are shopping for rotation help — CubsHQ trade rumors, ChiCitySports (fan reporting sites, not direct Hoyer quotes)
- **[LOW]** Casey Mize (Tigers) as Cubs trade target — ChiCitySports "surprising trade target"; MLB Trade Rumors Cuba Notes. Speculative — no confirmed deal
- **[LOW]** Reid Detmers (Angels) as Cubs trade target — CubbiesCrib "ideal fit"; Angels expected sellers. Speculative — no confirmed deal
- **[HIGH]** Cubs rotation is depleted: Steele (60-day IL), Boyd (delayed return), Taillon (IL since June 8), Horton (shut down) — FanGraphs injury report
research.sources: Source: ChiCitySports trade deadline articles; CubbiesCrib "Detmers" piece; MLBTradeRumors Cubs Notes; FanGraphs Roster Resource injury page

stories.title: Pete Crow-Armstrong — The Franchise's Bright Spot
stories.angle: PCA .263/.341/.462, 12 HR, 15 SB, 3.9 WAR (among MLB leaders per StatMuse). On a team with a collapsed rotation, he's the reason the Cubs remain in the wild card race. All-Star case building.

x_posts.label: Post 4A
x_posts.text:
.263/.341/.462. 12 home runs. 15 stolen bases. 3.9 WAR — among MLB's leaders.

Pete Crow-Armstrong is playing at an All-Star level. The national attention is overdue.

On a team with a busted rotation, PCA is holding this thing together.

#Cubs #GoCubs #MLB

research.facts:
Pete Crow-Armstrong — The Franchise's Bright Spot
- **[HIGH]** PCA batting line: .263/.341/.462, 12 HR, 15 SB — StatMuse 2026 season stats
- **[MEDIUM]** 3.9 WAR — StatMuse (cited as "leads Majors in WAR"). Medium confidence: WAR changes daily and secondary confirmation was not obtained. Tweet uses "among MLB's leaders" rather than "leads all of baseball" to avoid overclaim.
- **[HIGH]** 71 games played — StatMuse
- **[HIGH]** PCA is on an All-Star pace based on stats — editorial judgment backed by ChiCitySports article ("All-Star level since infamous exchange")
- **[MEDIUM]** Slow start (.229 in first 48 G), then surge (.329 with 6 HR in last 23 G) — StatMuse game log interpretation
research.sources: Source: StatMuse PCA 2026 season page; ChiCitySports PCA All-Star article; CBS Sports splits

stories.title: Wild Card Watch — The Brewers Ran Away, Now What?
stories.angle: Brewers 41-25, division lead insurmountable. Cubs 37-35 focused on wild card. Division rivals crowding the hunt. Every win against weak opposition matters.

x_posts.label: Post 5A
x_posts.text:
The Brewers are 41-25. The division is gone.

Cubs sit at 37-35 and locked into the wild card race. Not a concession — a pivot.

Division rivals are crowding the hunt. Every win vs. a basement team matters. Three against Colorado starts tonight.

#Cubs #NorthSiders #MLB

research.facts:
Wild Card Watch — Brewers Lead, Cubs Pivot
- **[HIGH]** Cubs record: 37-35 — series-context.json
- **[HIGH]** Brewers record: 41-25 (as of June 13-14) — BrewCrewBall, MLB.com Brewers standings
- **[MEDIUM]** Cardinals record ~30-26 — cross-referenced standings search (one result said 30-26, another said 37-29 which is implausible given context). Specific Cardinals record deliberately excluded from tweet to avoid error.
- **[HIGH]** Division title is out of reach for Cubs — derived from 6+ game deficit to Brewers in mid-June
- **[HIGH]** Cubs pursuing wild card — confirmed by prior story-history, series context
